text:
Admission Date:  2017-12-19     Discharge Date:  1971-01-16


Service:  MEDICAL INTENSIVE CARE UNIT

HISTORY OF THE PRESENT ILLNESS:  Mr. Todd Oliver presented
with delta MS Jason Clark.  The patient is a 79-year-old
gentleman with a history of CAD, status post CABG in 1971,
status post recent non-Q wave MI in 1994-05-08 also with
CHF with an EF of 20%, status post left femoral DP bypass
graft on 1998-08-01 complicated by a wound infection with VRE
and dehiscence which was treated with debridement, new muscle
flap, and linazolid, presented with mental status change for
three days described as lethargy, confusion, and
disorientation.

In the ED, the patient was found to have a fingerstick of 30
and a blood pressure of 100-110/40-60.  In the ED, he
received D50 and IV fluids.  He was also started on
Octreotide for possible Glyburide toxicity and Flagyl,
Levofloxacin, and vancomycin for possible bacteremia,
although there was no clear source.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
1.  CAD, status post CABG in 1971.
2.  CHF with an EF of 20%.
3.  Diabetes mellitus type 2.
4.  Peripheral vascular disease, status post femoral DP
bypass.
5.  Anemia.
6.  Diverticulitis.
7.  Depression.
8.  History of MRSA.
9.  Ventral hernia.
10. Appendectomy.
11. Colectomy.

ADMISSION MEDICATIONS:
1.  Carvedilol.
2.  Lisinopril.
3.  Nitroglycerin sublingual.
4.  Linazolid.
5.  Pantoprazole.
6.  Fluconazole.
7.  Lasix.
8.  Metoprolol.
9.  Glyburide.
10. Aspirin.

HOSPITAL COURSE:  The patient had a five day hospital course
in the Medical Intensive Care Unit.  During that time, his
blood cultures returned negative; however, was continued on
levofloxacin and Flagyl.  His urine output remained very
marginal even after having received IV fluids.  His
coagulations remained elevated as did his LFTs.  He had been
started on pressors which reversed his hypotension into
hypertension.  His glucose remained difficult to manage and
had to be started on a D50 drip.  Overall, his overall
condition was quite critical and his code status was DNI/DNR.

After a complicated hospital course, he passed away on
1971-01-16 at 9:00 a.m.




                          Ashley Butler David Garcia, M.D.  89000

Dictated By:Mcbride
